Key Takeaways:
• Your Sales Page is where your Sales Video or your Sales Letter (or a combo of the
two) will live.
• This is done during the Open Cart period – when you’re ready to start taking
orders.
• You need to have a strong sales video or sales letter that's going to draw people in
and get them to crack open their wallets.
• The really big thing to remember, right here we've got it, the number one thing to
remember is it's not about your stuff. It's not about what you're giving
them. It's about the transformation.
• Think about the avatar questions of transformation when developing your sales
message.
• The Quickstart Guide has a full framework for creating your sales message that
includes:
o Headline
o Subheadline
o Sales Message – the Big Promise or Big Hook
o Share the Opportunity and Transformation
o Share your authority
o Raise and answer objections
o Proof and Testimonials
o Foreshadow urgency
o Make your offer and cover benefits and features
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

o Deliverables
o Bonuses

o Payment terms
o Tell them what to do – call to action
o Scarcity

Okay so now let's talk about your sales page. This is where your sales video or
your sales letter or a combination of the two is going to live. This is once you've gone
through your Prelaunch, it's time to open cart and it's time to start taking orders. Yeah
baby, this is it so just to find yourself within the overall process, if you've checked out the
earlier Quickstarts, you know this diagram.
First we had the Pre-Pre then PLC 1, PLC 2 and PLC 3, the opportunity, the
transformation and the ownership. Now we're over here in launch land. This is where we
actually open cart. This is what we call this period. Once you've gone through your
Prelaunch, next comes Open Cart. During your Open Cart is when you have the sales
page.
Now the thing to remember is that in the perfect world, people will be completely
sold by your Prelaunch and they're just going to be standing in line with their credit card
ready to buy. If you've done everything right and you've really nailed your Prelaunch,
there's going to be some people like that but not everyone's going to be like that. That's
why you need to have a strong sales video or sales letter that's going to draw people in
and get them to crack open their wallets.
The really big thing to remember, right here we've got it, the number one thing to
remember is it's not about your stuff. It's not about what you're giving them. It's
about the transformation. I know you've put a lot of time and effort into your
product. You love your product. It's been a labor of love. It's been blood, sweat and tears.
You're proud of it but it's not about those videos. It’s not about that widget. It's not about
the seminar. It's not about the membership site. It's about how that's going to change
your prospect's life, your avatar's life. How is their life going to be different after they use
your product? That's what you need to convey to them in your sales message. It's way
more important than if they're going to get all these videos or these DVDs or they get to
come to a fancy hotel for three days or whatever. That's not nearly as important as what
they're going to get out of it.
Okay, so in terms of developing your sales message, this is where it comes from.
These questions will really put you in the state of really thinking about your avatar or
your prospect and how their life is going to change and how you want to communicate to
them so what's their biggest problem, their biggest struggle that they have? Why are they
having that problem? What is the problem costing them? I like to think about what's it
costing them right now? What's it costing them in three months? What's it costing them
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
in a year, in five years, in 30 years? When I'm selling Product Launch Formula, it's really
easy for me to think about all the people that are either about to start a business because
it's a Product Launch Formula or they're going to grow their business dramatically.
The thing is… is it's not like a one-time deal. It's not like you start a business and
it's, "Yay, oh I just made $10,000 or $100,000 or whatever," It's they're going to keep on
making that money next year and the year after and the year after and the year after.
They're going to work thriving business. It's not like someone's just going to come in and
they've got a business that's doing $150,000 a year and they're all of a sudden going to
add another $50,000 or $100,000 to their business. It's not like it's a one-time deal.
They're going to keep on doing that.
At least in your mind, you don't have to communicate all of this but as you're
getting ready to create your sales video or your sales level or the script for your video,
these are the things you want to think about to put you in the state of creating the best
most powerful message. What's this problem costing them? Why can't they solve the
problem on their own? What are some of the most common mistakes that people make
who are trying to solve this problem? What are some of the biggest misconceptions about
the problem? How would someone fix or solve this problem and how does your solution
tie in? Then how will their life be better and how will the people around them, their lives
be better? Their family members, their friends, their children, their grandchildren.
These questions - I'm just trying to get you to focus on your avatar and what their
problem is and how you could solve it. Now, I like to split things down into… people are
either avoiding pain - they're moving away from pain or they're moving towards pleasure
so they might not have this screaming problem but you're just simply going to make their
lives better. All these questions will still apply even in that case.
Here's a framework I like to use when I'm thinking about creating a sales
message, a sales video, a sales script, a sales letter. First of all, you want to start off with a
headline. In the headline, you're typically describing the big benefit. How is their life
going to change? Then you want to sub head in general to describe the problem and the
main benefit of the solution. Now if you it as a video, you want to think in terms of the
headline's going to be sort of that first big statement that you make. You might not have
the classical headline like you would see in a sales letter but what is that first big promise
that you're going to make in your sales video?
Then as we get down into the sales message, what's the big promise, the big
hook? What's in it for the viewer? What's in it for your avatar, your prospect? Then you
want to share the opportunity and a transformation. Now this is a lot of the work you've
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 8 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
already one in PLC 1 and PLC 2. What is that big opportunity? What's the journey?
How's their life going to change? Are they going to be able to play songs on the guitar?
Are they going to be able to take X number of strokes off their golf game? Are they going
to be able to build a meditation practice? How is their life going to change?
Then you want to share your authority because without authority, it's genuinely
hard to sell. Authority is what makes people believe you. Then you want to think about
proof and testimonials. This is going to take up the bulk or at least the first 50% of your
sales video or your sales letter or your sales script. Then you want to foreshadow
urgency. You're going to foreshadow the idea of, "This is going to be a limited offer,"
because your open cart period is only going to be for a certain period of time and after
open curt period ends then the deal they get is not going to be as good or they might not
even be able to get the deal. You want to just foreshadow that.
Then you want to make your offer and this is where you cover the benefits and
the features. What the product does, who it's for, how it's going to transform your
prospect's life for the better. What makes it different from other products in the market
and in this case, generally, I'm not a big fan of comparing my product to other products
head to head. I don't and they actually take you down the road of criticizing other
products, not a big fan. Not a big fan, I like to, by this point in the launch if someone's
still with you. They've gone through the PLC, they're still with you, they have at some
level bought into your ability to solve their problem. In that case, I sort of want to sell
like I'm the only solution in the marketplace. If you are going to compare in here then I
tend to do it somewhat obliquely. It's not like I'm actually naming the other products.
I'm just talking about the things that make my product a superior solution to anything
out there.
Then there's how does it work? This is some details. There could be a story
around how you discovered this or how you made it, how it came to be, the origin story.
You can give some statistics, some proof, some of the unique characteristics, why it was
created in the first place, what are the deliverables? By deliverables, I mean this is what
people actually get. I stressed very strongly a few minutes ago how it's about the
transformation.
It's not about the stuff but at some point you do have to talk about what the stuff
actually is. What's the forum that it takes? Is it online video? Is it DVDs? Is it a widget?
What is it? You know, what is it? This is something that comes after you've covered the
transformation and when you're talking about it, if you can build in the transformation
that every piece allows, so much the better.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Then just the details of how and when they actually get it. How it's going to be
delivered. Then you want to cover any bonuses and I love to add in bonuses. This is an
advanced thing. I love to add in bonuses that answer objections. For instance, for
Product Launch Formula, one of the biggest objections is, "I don't have a list," so I
created a list building course and that comes as a bonus with Product Launch Formula.
The beautiful thing about this is that bonus answers the objection plus as you're talking
about the bonus, you get to answer the objections.
Then you want to reveal price and this is actually something that should've been
up in here that I forgot to put in here, my apologies, that you want to put in here and it's
going to be basically right in here, probably before the proof and testimonials is you want
to raise and answer the objections. You're doing that all the way through Prelaunch. You
also have to do it in your sales message as well.
The price reveal comes down here. It's after you've built all the value. If you walk
onto a Ferrari dealership, they don't have the price blazed on ... It's not in huge numbers
on the windshield. That's just not the way it works. With a higher end sale, you have to
establish the value before you reveal the price and that's what you absolutely have to do.
You want to talk about your price comparison, justification. For less than a cup of coffee
a day, that's what this is going to cost you. This is a place where you have to cover in
detail what are the payment methods, if there's a payment plan, what that payment plan
is, how often the payments are do and for how long and how much they are.
Then you tell them what to do, click the button, fill out the form and then this will
happen. In here, this is where you deliver on the scarcity and the urgency. There can be
all kinds of different ways to do this. You could have a quantity limit like on how many
are available, physical limit on how many available. If you've got a seminar, it's just
natural that seminar's going to come and go and there's probably going to be a limited
number of seats. If it’s a physical product, maybe you only have certain number
available. It could be a workload limit. You can only work with so many people at a time.
You could do fast action bonuses. The first X number of customers get this extra
bonus. I'm not a big fan of fast action bonuses because I think it tends to irritate the
people that didn't get in on the fast action. It's something that we used to do a lot more
years ago. I stopped doing it long ago. I probably stopped doing it in like 2006 or
something like that so I'm not a huge fan. Some people still like to use that.
Then what we use in Product Launch Formula is a schedule limit. We run a class
so class is about to get started so get enrolled. Class is starting on Monday. Then you can
just have a deadline that we are pulling the product off the market. In general, when
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

8
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
you're talking about urgency, there's three big ones that it sort of falls into is that either
the product is coming off the market, the price is going up or bonuses are going away.
Those are the three real big ones and again product is coming off the market, price is
going up, bonuses are going away.
The most effective of those is the product is coming off the market but you might
not want to pull your product off the market but the thing is - at the end of your Open
Cart period, something has to happen so the deal is not as good after that Open Cart
period. Absolutely critical so then after you go through the urgency, whatever it is and it
could be a combination of any of these, anything I mentioned. It doesn't have to be just
one.
Then you have your call to action which is you ask for the sale. You ask them to go
ahead and click the buy button. I do have here you can test different text for the actual
order button and that can definitely impact your conversions so that's a good thing to
test. Then at this point, I like to throw in a surprise bonus. Actually, usually what I'll do
here is I'll reverse these. I'll go with risk reversal and then a surprise bonus so what I'll do
is a call to action then I'll do the risk reversal which is a guarantee and then come out
with a surprise bonus then a re-close.
You don't have to do the surprise bonus. I don't always do that but you absolutely
have to do the call to action, the risk reversal and then a re-close. This is where you
revisit that scarcity and why they have to act right now. If you want to after that you can
come in with some more social proof which could be testimonials, blog comments, the
number of people who bought, the results they've gotten, anything like that but then if
you do that, you do want to come in always at the end a final call to action, a final call to
order. That should always be the last thing out of your mouth when you go to close your
video, close your sales site.
That is sort of a look at a lot of the elements and an overall framework. We do
have down here, I've got a checklist for you. I'm not going to go through that. We've
basically already been through most of that but there's your sales page checklist.
